Life Physics

Repost: Midterm season

January 19, 2026

This is the time of the year when midterms are around. I had it for past 2 years, but it's my first time taking 5 midterms in 3 days. I just picked up too many hard courses so I have to take so many.

Good thing is that my physics em class is mostly covered from me studying hrk, and econ is easy I could probably just study like 3 hrs for it. Also my lang class is doing some sort of in class essay, so I don't really need to prepare for that since the prompt is given at the moment I arrive. Now the problem is, I have a calc and history midterm on the same day. Hardest classes, and I'm sure going to fail them.

Anyways, the reason I am not studying for midterms is because our robotics competition is coming up, and our bot isn't built yet. I finished Cadding yesterday. I also ordered in stuff, and watched a lot of gameplay to train myself (definitely not for entertainment). So therefore my 2 days of weekend is gone, and I'm absolutely done for. It's not just midterms, and there's still some tests I have to study for BEFORE the midterms.

I really want to study physics and do hrk because I am really late on schedule, still not finishing the concepts when I should be doing mocks. On the good note, week after the midterm would be free and I would be able to dedicate my time for physics. gee I have robotics and math counts volunteering so let me just cancel my previous sentence.

The reason I am taking so long is mostly because of my poor time management, but I learn slowly and I repeat until I understand everything. Physics is pretty cool since it actually applies to real life, unlike my previous genre number theory, which is just simply cool numbers. And yeah physics is pretty fun. I'm not really used to hrk problems being simple and theoretical and suddenly one problem being some massive algebraic manipulation, but I heard blue morin is better so I should use those too. I only searched up few f=ma problems, but they seem to be kind of tricky but easy if you know what it is. Not sure how I would do.

Comments
